subject: LU-5541 lustreapi: only export the API symbols

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/26280/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Unfortunately, this is not one of my stronger areas, so I'll have to defer to others who know more about it than I do.

Patch Set 1:

This file gets updated manually. It's hard to miss because if a symbol isn't exported, the linker won't find it, and linking with an application will fail. 


This mechanism is used by all openfabrics infiniband libraries (like https://kernel.googlesource.com/pub/scm/libs/infiniband/libibverbs/+/9a2a8962da24ad3f232a4703ad00d89a1443648e/src/libibverbs.map)

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

My personal preference would maybe go to exposing all llapi_ functions and use non llapi_-prefixed functions internally. This sounds easy to maintain and it makes it especially obvious what is a public API and what is not.

That said, as long as we stick to something consistent I don't see much difference between both options...

parents.subject: LU-5541 build: build static and dynamic liblustreapi

commit.subject: LU-5541 lustreapi: only export the API symbols
commit.message:
LU-5541 lustreapi: only export the API symbols

By default, all kind of symbols are exported from the library (dump,
libcfs_ukuc_start, l_ioctl, set_ioctl_dump, ...), which may create
external conflicts. Use the linker version-script options to only
export the API symbols, and prevent the export of internal symbols.

Only the symbols declared in the global section of liblustreapi.map
will be seen by applications.

Fix lshowmount to use libcfs and not internal liblustreapi symbol.

Change-Id: Ica4226c1ea9b6b159a056ad22bacaa2ffcf4b171
